litigation.mdlName: In re: Social Media Adolescent Addiction/Personal Injury Products Liability Litigation
litigation.court: U.S. District Court, Northern District of California

phaseDetail: MDL-3047 is in active litigation with bellwether trials proceeding in 2026, alongside a parallel California state-court coordination (JCCP 5255) in Los Angeles. There is no global settlement; defendants have reached only confidential settlements in individual bellwether cases.

pendingCounts.source: JPML, Pending MDL Dockets by Actions Pending, June 1, 2026

settlements.label: No global settlement — confidential individual bellwether deals only
settlements.detail: There is no global or program settlement in MDL-3047 or the California JCCP. Some defendants have reached confidential settlements in individual bellwether cases: in the first Los Angeles JCCP personal-injury bellwether, a jury awarded $6 million in March 2026 against Meta and YouTube, while Snap and TikTok had settled that case before trial; ahead of the second personal-injury bellwether set for July 27, 2026, YouTube reached a confidential settlement in June 2026. No amount is guaranteed for any individual claim.
settlements.source: Law.com (The Recorder) — YouTube settles second bellwether (June 24, 2026)

keyDates.label: MDL-3047 established
keyDates.detail: The JPML centralized federal social-media adolescent-injury cases before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California.

keyDates.label: Federal school-district bellwether trial begins
keyDates.detail: The MDL's school-district bellwether (Breathitt County, Kentucky) went to trial in June 2026; school-district and personal-injury plaintiffs allege harms from addictive platform design.

keyDates.label: Second Los Angeles JCCP personal-injury bellwether set
keyDates.detail: A second personal-injury bellwether is scheduled in the California JCCP; YouTube settled out in June 2026, while Meta, Snap, and TikTok remain defendants.
